configs.name: A · Four On Top
configs.tagline: Six tables; four rounds + a center fifth on the north end

anomalies.where: center-east: walk crosses a wall two-top

notes: The four aligned rounds and center fifth double as a tournament gallery — they face the nearest table row.
Wall two-tops at every table end flex between drinkers, eaters, and spectators; stools face the tops.
FLAG: The center round of the north quincunx runs tight to its four neighbours — deliberate, for one big social cluster.
FLAG: The two-top by the kitchen door partially fronts it (accepted).
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 60"; the shortest clearance is 56.5".
pros: The four aligned rounds and center fifth double as a tournament gallery — they face the nearest table row.

cons: The center round of the north quincunx runs tight to its four neighbours — deliberate, for one big social cluster.
The two-top by the kitchen door partially fronts it (accepted).
configs.measured: Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 60"; the shortest clearance is 56.5".

configs.name: B · Four On Top — Turned
configs.tagline: The Four On Top pattern with all six tables rotated 90°

anomalies.where: south-center: walk crosses a wall two-top
south-east: walk crosses a wall two-top

notes: The Four On Top pattern turned sideways: four tables in a block at the top, two at the bottom, every table rotated.
Each cluster gets its own two-top band — top wall, between the clusters, and below the bottom pair — so every table has seating at arm's reach.
FLAG: the rotated ends run tight side-to-side — the west and center aisles are the practical maximum, not the cue standard.
FLAG: The south-wall tops narrow the along-wall walk; servers pass north of them.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 47"; the shortest clearance is 46.8".
pros: The Four On Top pattern turned sideways: four tables in a block at the top, two at the bottom, every table rotated.

configs.measured: Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 47"; the shortest clearance is 46.8".

configs.name: C · Four On Top — Turned Left
configs.tagline: The turned pattern slid left: play opens from the east

anomalies.where: south-center: walk crosses a wall two-top
south-east: walk crosses a wall two-top

notes: The turned four-and-two slides left: the whole east side becomes service and standing room with full end swings.
FLAG: the west end swings run hard against the west wall — the cost of the slide.
Two-top bands as in the centered version, shifted with the tables.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 47"; the shortest clearance is 37.2".
pros: The turned four-and-two slides left: the whole east side becomes service and standing room with full end swings.

cons: The west end swings run hard against the west wall — the cost of the slide.
configs.measured: Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 47"; the shortest clearance is 37.2".

configs.name: D · Four On Top — Turned Right
configs.tagline: The turned pattern slid right: play opens from the west

anomalies.where: south-center: walk crosses a wall two-top
south-east: walk crosses a wall two-top

notes: The turned four-and-two slides right as far as the chase and entry well allow; the west side becomes service and standing room with full end swings.
FLAG: the east end swings run hard toward the east wall — the cost of the slide.
Two-top bands as in the centered version, shifted with the tables.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 47"; the shortest clearance is 37.2".

configs.name: E · Center Line
configs.tagline: Six tables turned 90°, end-to-end down the middle
notes: Every table on display: the side aisles run the full length, so one walk covers all six tables.
FLAG: Side-to-side swings between neighbouring tables run tight; the table ends get full room.
FLAG: West-rail drink deliveries cross the line at every table.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 74"; the shortest clearance is 54.8".
pros: Every table on display: the side aisles run the full length, so one walk covers all six tables.
cons: Side-to-side swings between neighbouring tables run tight; the table ends get full room.
West-rail drink deliveries cross the line at every table.
configs.measured: Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 74"; the shortest clearance is 54.8".

configs.name: F · East Line + West Lounge
configs.tagline: Tables single-file on the east; full-length hospitality strip west
notes: The west side becomes a 9-ft hospitality strip the full length of the room — six rounds in one line, each on its table's centerline.
FLAG: the sixth (south) round partially narrows the Emergency Exit approach — the corridor bends around it.
FLAG: Same tight side-to-side swings as the center line; table ends get full room.
FLAG: Every delivery crosses the table line — the price of putting all hospitality opposite the doors.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 55"; the shortest clearance is 54.8".

cons: The sixth (south) round partially narrows the Emergency Exit approach — the corridor bends around it.

configs.measured: Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 55"; the shortest clearance is 54.8".

configs.name: G · East Line — Shifted Down
configs.tagline: The east line slid down-wall: the top table rejoins the line
notes: The whole line slides toward the bottom wall: the top table rejoins the straight line and every compromise at the entry end dissolves.
All six rounds sit on their tables' centerlines at the west wall, fully clear of the Emergency Exit approach.
FLAG: the bottom table's end swing runs tight against the bottom wall — the cost of the slide.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 55"; the shortest clearance is 43.0".
pros: The whole line slides toward the bottom wall: the top table rejoins the straight line and every compromise at the entry end dissolves.

cons: The bottom table's end swing runs tight against the bottom wall — the cost of the slide.

configs.name: H · West Line + Wall Rounds
configs.tagline: Tables single-file west; a round beside each table on the east wall

anomalies.where: south-east: walk crosses a round's chairs
center-east: between a round's chairs and a round's chairs

notes: Each of the six tables gets its own 5-ft round at matching height on the east side — table service at arm's reach.
FLAG: the sixth (south) round crowds the Main Entry approach beside the stair rail.
The kitchen-door table and its round nudge left, clearing the door frontage entirely.
FLAG: that table's inboard swing tightens — the cost of clearing the door.
FLAG: Another round pulls inboard of the HVAC chase.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 55"; the shortest clearance is 39.3".

configs.name: I · West Line — Shifted Down
configs.tagline: The west line slid down-wall: the sixth round reaches the wall

anomalies.where: south-east: walk crosses a round's chairs
center-east: between a round's chairs and a round's chairs

notes: The whole line slides toward the bottom wall, freeing the entry end: the sixth round reaches the east wall like the rest instead of pulling far inboard.
FLAG: the sixth round's chairs still graze the Main Entry approach corner (accepted).
The kitchen-door table and its round nudge left, clearing the door frontage entirely and keeping the wall approach open.
FLAG: kitchen service still threads a tight squeeze between the third and fourth rounds' chairs.
FLAG: The fourth round pulls inboard of the HVAC chase; the first pulls inboard of the storage-door frontage.
FLAG: the bottom table's end swing runs tight against the bottom wall — the cost of the slide.
Cue room around the tables: most sides get about 55"; the shortest clearance is 41.0".
